channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 90 days.

item.title: Queer|Art Design Grant 2026 (Inaugural)
item.description:
Organisation: Queer|Art (in partnership with UGG)

Location: United States; open to US-based emerging queer/LGBTQ+ designers.

Award: Two USD 10,000 grants (described as the largest cash grant for LGBTQ+ designers in the US). No application fee.

Duration: One-off grant; winners announced at Queer|Art's Annual Party, Fall 2026

Deadline: 14 Aug 2026

The Queer|Art Design Grant is a new initiative from Queer|Art supporting projects that explore queer histories, legacies, and futures while providing visibility and opportunity for emerging LGBTQ+ design talent. AWARDS: two USD 10,000 grants to emerging queer creatives across the design industry, described as the largest cash grant for LGBTQ+ designers in the country. ELIGIBILITY: US-based designers working across a variety of disciplines, including graphic design, architecture and landscape architecture, industrial and product design, fashion, textile and wearables, digital design, service design, furniture and home decor, materials research, interior design, and more. In its inaugural year the grant is judged by a panel of designers, curators, architects, and cultural leaders, including Bex McCharen, Salome Asega, Cristina Grajales, Jaffer Kolb, and Paul Soulellis. The program is the first major initiative under a new partnership between UGG and Queer|Art; Queer|Art board member and creative strategist Fran Tirado serves as Community Curator. Winners will be announced at Queer|Art's Annual Party in Fall 2026. DEADLINE: 14 August 2026. Apply via https://www.queer-art.org/design-grant.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Emergence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: New Emergence Art

Location: International; open worldwide. Shortlist shown in an online exhibition.

Award: Prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize cash award, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee.

Duration: One-off prize; shortlisted artists shown in an online exhibition 11 August to 7 September 2026

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The New Emergence Art Prize 2026 is an international open call for emerging artists, presented by New Emergence Art. There is no set theme and work may be submitted in any medium. AWARDS: a prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards supporting artists through cash prizes, artist materials, mentoring, and a residency, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to emerging artists; entrants may submit up to three original artworks that represent the quality, originality and direction of their practice. Final selections are made by an independent judging panel considering concept, execution, originality and impact. Shortlisted artists are featured in an online exhibition running 11 August to 7 September 2026. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee (amount set at the entry step). DEADLINE: 3 August 2026, 23:59 BST. Apply via https://www.newemergenceart.com/apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Create NSW: Stories Matter NSW Literary Fellowships 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Create NSW (administered by Writing NSW)

Location: New South Wales, Australia; applicants must be NSW-based.

Award: AUD 50,000 per fellowship, 10 fellowships awarded (4 emerging, 3 mid-career, 3 established). No application fee.

Duration: 12-month fellowship

Deadline: 27 Jul 2026

Stories Matter is a new NSW literary fellowship program from Create NSW, administered by Writing NSW, awarding ten fellowships of AUD 50,000 each to support NSW-based writers to focus on a major new work over a 12-month period. The 2026 round funds four emerging, three mid-career, and three established writers working across prose fiction, poetry, illustration, graphic novels, and playwriting. ELIGIBILITY: writers must be based in New South Wales; open to writers at any career stage within the three tiers (not open to applicants outside NSW). No application fee. DEADLINE: 27 July 2026, 2:00pm AEST. Apply via the Create NSW grants portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Heart2World Publishing: Heart2World Book Prize 2026 (Inaugural, Poetry)
item.description:
Organisation: Heart2World Publishing

Location: Nigeria; open to Nigerian writers currently residing in Nigeria.

Award: NGN 250,000 cash prize, a full publishing and marketing package (valued at approximately NGN 5 million), and 100 percent net royalties on the published collection. No entry or reading fee.

Duration: One-off prize; publication to follow

Deadline: 05 Aug 2026

The inaugural Heart2World Book Prize, from Heart2World Publishing, is a poetry prize for a full-length collection by a Nigerian writer. AWARD: NGN 250,000 cash prize, a full publishing and marketing package (valued at approximately NGN 5 million), and 100 percent net royalties on the published collection. ELIGIBILITY: Nigerian citizens currently residing in Nigeria; submit an unpublished poetry collection of 30 to 35 poems written in English. There is no entry or reading fee (verified on the funder page). DEADLINE: 5 August 2026. Apply via the prize page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fondazione Prada Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fondazione Prada

Location: Milan, Italy-based fund; open worldwide to independent producers and production companies.

Award: EUR 1.5 million total pool supporting 10 to 12 feature films. Development grants EUR 15,000 to 80,000; production grants EUR 30,000 to 250,000; post-production grants EUR 40,000 to 100,000. No application fee.

Duration: Feature film funding (development, production, or post-production)

Deadline: 06 Aug 2026

The Fondazione Prada Film Fund is an annual EUR 1.5 million fund backing 10 to 12 feature-length films (fiction, documentary, and animation, 65 minutes or longer) across development, production, and post-production. ELIGIBILITY: independent producers and production companies with a track record (approximately two or more feature films premiered at major international festivals) and partial financing already secured; this is aimed at established independent producers, NOT individual emerging artists. DEADLINES: 6 August 2026 for projects in production; 10 September 2026 for development and post-production projects (this card uses the earliest deadline). Apply via the Fondazione Prada Film Fund page. NOTE: the funder's primary page was not directly reachable at time of entry; dates corroborated via industry trade press (Screen, Cineuropa). Verify the exact track deadline before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Inside Out RE:Focus Fund Post-Production Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Inside Out (Toronto 2SLGBTQ+ Film Festival)

Location: Toronto, Canada (Inside Out is a Canadian organisation; international projects may apply, see FAQ)

Award: Post-production grant funding to support the completion and promotion of short and feature films; the requested amount is set by the applicant (see the FAQ 'How much funding can I request?'). Since 2018 the RE:Focus Fund has distributed more than CAD 300,000 in grants and programs. No application fee.

Duration: One-off post-production grant

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

Established in 2018 through a lead gift from Martha McCain, Inside Out's RE:Focus Fund addresses industry inequities by providing direct financial support to women, non-binary, and/or trans filmmakers telling 2SLGBTQ+ stories. Launched in 2019, the RE:Focus Fund Post-Production Grants support the post-production and promotion of short and feature films. Applications for the 2026 round open 29 June 2026. ELIGIBILITY: women, non-binary, and/or trans filmmakers whose projects tell 2SLGBTQ+ stories; per the FAQ, international projects may be eligible to apply. The requested funding amount is determined by the applicant, and unsuccessful applicants can request feedback and reapply the following year. Inside Out also runs other RE:Focus initiatives, including a new Residency program for first-time feature filmmakers facing intersectional barriers. KEY DATES: submissions open 29 June 2026, close 22 July 2026, with notifications by 15 August 2026. Apply and read the FAQ via the Eventival page linked from the RE:Focus Fund site; questions to industry@insideout.ca.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AIxDESIGN x KB: Open Call for Sessions, (Art)works and Performances
item.description:
Organisation: AIxDESIGN, co-presented with the Koninklijke Bibliotheek (KB), National Library of the Netherlands

Location: KB, The Hague, Netherlands (in-person exhibition and programme); pitches welcome from people based anywhere

Award: Fees by track: opening-night experience EUR 1,500 to 2,000; a session in the events programme EUR 2,000 to 2,500; an existing (art)work for the exhibition EUR 200 to 400 honorarium (including licensing and production). The fee stays the same wherever you are based.

Duration: Exhibition runs November 2026 to February 2027; opening event November 2026

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

AIxDESIGN and the KB (Koninklijke Bibliotheek, the National Library of the Netherlands) are building an exhibition and public programme about AI, made from the national collection, at the KB in The Hague. Nadia Piet is Creative Lead with the AIxDESIGN community, co-presented with the KB. The premise: long before neural nets, libraries were the machines we built to organise human knowledge, and the show uses the KB's collection to look at what AI is, where it comes from, and how it connects to the older story of how we store, sort, and search what we know. Part of the show lives in the KB Atelier, with talks, workshops, and screenings alongside, intended as AI literacy you can wander into. WHAT THEY WANT: work that engages AI in some way (critical, creative, hands-on, or playful); bonus if it also speaks to archives, libraries, or how we store and sort knowledge, though you do not need to touch the KB's collection to take part. You may pitch to more than one track: (A) SOMETHING FOR THE OPENING NIGHT, a performance, talk, screening, tour, ritual, booth, game, or other experience for the November 2026 opening, fee EUR 1,500 to 2,000; (B) A SESSION IN THE EVENTS PROGRAMME, a workshop or other session once during the three-month run, around 2.5 to 4 hours, fee EUR 2,000 to 2,500; (C) AN (ART)WORK FOR THE EXHIBITION, existing work in any format (installation, sculpture, zine stand, a corner of your own) sturdy enough for a public area with daily traffic, honorarium EUR 200 to 400 including licensing and production; (D) SOMETHING ELSE ENTIRELY, pitch it anyway. WHO: artists, designers, researchers, technologists, performers, musicians, archivists, or educators working around AI, data, libraries, or cultural heritage; no finished idea or polished CV needed, just a clear, specific pitch. You can be based anywhere and they especially welcome people outside the Netherlands; the show is in-person in The Hague and the fee stays the same wherever you are coming from. HOW TO APPLY: send a short pitch through the form (which track, your idea in a paragraph or two, roughly what it would need, and a link to your work); takes about five minutes, no full application required. Deadline: end of August 2026. Questions to hello@aixdesign.co.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Singapore AI Safety Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Singapore AI Safety Hub (SASH)

Location: Singapore (three-month in-person fellowship, 21 September - 4 December 2026)

Award: Stipend of SGD 5,000 per month, plus housing and travel. Up to USD 30,000 in compute per project. Includes mentorship, research management support, and central office space in Chinatown.

Duration: Three months in Singapore, 21 September to 4 December 2026

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

The Singapore AI Safety Fellowship is an independent research fellowship that connects fellows with leading mentors working on technical AI safety and governance. Fellows conduct research for three months in Singapore (21 September to 4 December 2026), building the skills and relationships for international coordination on AI safety. It is built for researchers who want their work to inform real-world AI safety practice on top of publishing it, drawing on Singapore's role as a globally connected, policy-engaged hub. The program rests on three pillars: cross-regional collaboration (bringing together researchers from East and West to build trusted relationships for sustained cross-border AI safety collaboration); technical depth and research excellence (guided by experienced researchers and advisors, fellows investigate pressing challenges in making frontier AI systems safe, secure, and trustworthy); and applying research to policy (institutional partnerships that ground projects in real-world application and help fellows translate technical ideas into practical frameworks and policy recommendations). Advisors include Zhang Ya-Qin (Chair Professor, Tsinghua University; Founding Dean of the Institute for AI Industry Research) and Ryan Kidd (Co-Executive Director, MATS Program). Fellows receive weekly mentorship from established AI safety researchers, dedicated Research Managers, a stipend of SGD 5,000 per month with housing provided throughout, up to USD 30,000 in compute for projects that require it, central office space at SASH's hub in Chinatown, and a community of talks, workshops, and social events. The application deadline is 10 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Upe Foundation Curatorial Fellowship at Camden Art Centre 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Upe Foundation (in partnership with Camden Art Centre)

Location: London, United Kingdom. Full-time, in-person; relocation to London is required. Applicants must hold the right to work in the UK.

Award: Salary of GBP 30,960 per year. A full-time Assistant Curator role on a 12-month fixed-term contract, with the possibility of a further 6-month extension (18 months total) subject to formal review and mutual agreement.

Duration: 12-month fixed-term position starting Autumn 2026, with possible 6-month extension (18 months total).

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Upe Foundation is launching the Upe Foundation Curatorial Fellowship at Camden Art Centre as part of its Fall 2026 Curatorial Fellowship Programme. The opportunity invites Baltic early-career curators to apply for a full-time curatorial role at Camden Art Centre, an institution internationally acclaimed for its rigorous exhibitions, residencies, events and learning programmes. The fellowship is a full-time, 12-month position starting Autumn 2026, with the possibility of a further 6-month extension (18 months total) upon review and mutual decision; relocation to London is required. ROLE: embedded within Camden Art Centre's Exhibitions Department, the Fellow works as Assistant Curator, supporting the development, coordination and delivery of the exhibitions programme and related projects and events, with practical experience across exhibition research, production, interpretation, publications, public programmes, loans, installation and documentation, and liaising with artists, galleries, lenders, partner organisations, writers and designers. The Fellow also shares research connected to Baltic and broader Central Eastern European artistic contexts. ELIGIBILITY: a demonstrated connection to or sustained engagement with the Baltic region (Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia, or their diasporas), which may include lived experience, heritage, working knowledge of a Baltic language, relevant research, or curatorial work with Baltic artists/institutions/contexts; early-career curatorial experience (freelance, self-organised, project-based or institutional); and a higher education qualification (BA, MA or equivalent) in curating, art history, visual culture or related disciplines. Desirable: working knowledge of Lithuanian, Latvian or Estonian; fluency in a second language; experience with Articheck and with CRM systems (particularly Salesforce). Applicants must hold the right to work in the UK (via the under-26 'new entrant' Skilled Worker route, the Creative Worker visa route, or existing right to work such as British citizenship or settled/pre-settled status). OFFER: GBP 30,960 salary, structured professional development within the Exhibitions team, and opportunities to build professional networks in London and internationally. Applications reviewed jointly by Upe Foundation and Camden Art Centre, with first-round online interviews and a second interview for finalists. The organizers strongly encourage applicants to write materials in their own words and avoid generic or AI-generated responses. TIMELINE: application deadline 12:00 noon UK time, Monday 13 July 2026; interviews 6 August 2026; final decision mid-August 2026; start Autumn 2026. Questions: recruitment@camdenartcentre.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AIxDESIGN: Grain, Open Call for Critical AI Literacy Recipes
item.description:
Organisation: AIxDESIGN, co-produced with the Netherlands Film Academy

Location: Remote/online; open to educators and practitioners based anywhere

Award: Need-based fee of EUR 150 per recipe for freelance and independent practitioners (email hello@aixdesign.co if the time would be hard to make without it). Your recipe is formatted and credited to you in the first edition of Grain, plus a print copy and an invite to the launch. Published under CC BY-SA 4.0; you keep authorship.

Duration: One-off contribution; Grain publishes March 2027

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Grain is a cookbook of critical and creative AI literacy exercises for educators across audiovisual and creative practice, co-produced by AIxDESIGN and the Netherlands Film Academy and led by Nadia Piet. The open call collects 'recipes': tried-and-tested group exercises for the classroom or workshop that get people thinking critically and making creatively with AI. Exercises can be hands-on or analog, 15 minutes or a full day, beginner or advanced; the one hard requirement is that someone else can read your recipe and run it themselves. Examples of what might fit: hands-on experiments with specific AI tools (image generators, chatbots, code assistants, video models); analog activities that reveal computational logic through embodied practice; critical readings, watchings, or listenings with structured discussion; provocations, games, or role-plays that get at how AI sees, thinks, or makes; and exercises that help people figure out how they want to relate to AI in their own practice. WHAT YOU GET: your recipe formatted and credited to you in the first edition of Grain, a print copy when it lands, and an invite to the launch. Everything is published under CC BY-SA 4.0 so the work can travel and you keep authorship. FEE: this round offers a need-based fee for freelance and independent practitioners of EUR 150 per recipe; if making the time would be hard without it, email hello@aixdesign.co with your proposal. After this round the call stays open but the fee will not. LIVE SESSION: an optional 30-minute live session on Wednesday 12 August 2026 at 16:00 CEST lets you bring a rough exercise and turn it into a recipe on the spot with the Grain Recipe Converter (online, sign up at luma.com/84w148in). Submissions are open until the end of August 2026; Grain publishes March 2027. Submit via the form; questions to hello@aixdesign.co.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Open Call 2027: Galerie mladych (Galerie TIC Brno)
item.description:
Organisation: Galerie TIC Brno

Location: Brno, Czech Republic. Exhibition projects realized at Galerie mladych during 2027.

Award: The gallery covers the financial costs of exhibition installation, an artist/curator fee, collection and transport of the exhibition, and basic PR (photodocumentation included).

Duration: Exhibition project for the year 2027.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Galerie TIC announces an open call for exhibition projects for Galerie mladych (Gallery of Young Artists) for the year 2027. Applicants should send a completed application form, a project description with visual documentation, and an artist's portfolio with CV by midnight on 20 July 2026 via the online form. THE GALLERY OFFERS: to cover the financial costs of exhibition installation; an artist/curator fee; collection and transport of the exhibition; and basic PR (photodocumentation included). APPLICATION: the application form, a short description and visual documentation of the artistic/curatorial project, and a portfolio with CV. Results will be published on the gallery's website and social media no later than the end of August 2026. Deadline 20 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Atheras: Nicosia Ludic Lab for Composers, Toyful Music Festival 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Atheras Peoples and Cultures

Location: Nicosia, Cyprus; open to individual artists based in a Creative Europe country

Award: Full financial support: travel, shared accommodation in central Nicosia, and daily allowance. Rehearsal space and full production support provided; final live concert recorded (video and audio) for documentation and international visibility.

Duration: 31-day residency, 23 October to 22 November 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Atheras Peoples and Cultures invites composers and performer-researchers to apply for the Nicosia Ludic Lab, a 31-day international residency dedicated to exploring 'play' as a compositional and performative methodology in contemporary music. Building on the artistic vision of the Toyful Music Festival, the residency creates a focused environment where experimentation, precision, and imagination coexist, redefining how music is conceived, structured, and performed. The residency runs in Nicosia, Cyprus, from 23 October to 22 November 2026, and participants develop new ensemble works that directly shape the artistic programme of the Toyful Music Festival 2026. ELIGIBILITY: applications are open to individual artists (proposals from duets or collectives will not be considered) based in a Creative Europe country, who must demonstrate a strong interest in experimental, ensemble-based practices. SELECTED RESIDENTS RECEIVE: full financial support (travel, accommodation, daily allowance); shared accommodation in central Nicosia; continuous access to a professional ensemble; mentorship sessions and individual meetings with ensemble members; coaching sessions with composer and conductor Andys Skordis; artistic and professional guidance from the Atheras team; rehearsal space and full production support for Phase 3; and video and audio recording of the final live concert for documentation and international visibility. Deadline: 15 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RAMDAM, un centre d'art: Extended Possibilities Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: RAMDAM, un centre d'art

Location: Sainte-Foy-les-Lyon (near Lyon), France; open to artists legally resident in Culture Moves Europe eligible countries (EU member states, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply

Award: EUR 1,350 per resident allowance for subsistence and other costs, plus travel allowance of EUR 400 (under 5,000 km) or EUR 800 (from 5,000 km). Top-ups: green mobility EUR 400 per resident (600 km or more by non-plane transport), visa EUR 120, outermost regions/OCT EUR 175, family EUR 200 per child under 18. On-site accommodation and technical facilities provided. Supported by Culture Moves Europe.

Duration: 45 days, 1 February to 15 March 2027

Deadline: 14 Jul 2026

Extended Possibilities gives artists the opportunity to work on site at RAMDAM, un centre d'art for 45 days, a long period allowing them to benefit from technical support and to present ongoing work to the public. Artists receive on-site accommodation (a collective room for the whole 45 days), access to a well-maintained, up-to-date technical facility, a grant to support their work, and a lump-sum allowance for travel and visas. RAMDAM is an activist space supporting artists in their artistic research; residencies are open to artists or teams engaged in creation, research, or experimentation, with no obligation to produce a finished work: dance, theatre, performance, painting, sculpture, drawing, film, video, literary writing, screenwriting, digital art, and hybrid forms are all welcome. The call focuses on multidisciplinary projects and research that break down barriers between disciplines and challenge their own field of practice, and considerable attention is given to how the work fits into a social and/or political context and resonates with our times. THE CALL SUPPORTS: 1 or 2 artistic teams (RAMDAM can host at most 5 artists); performing artists of all disciplines; projects at early stages of research-creation and/or more advanced (residencies can be writing residencies and/or stage residencies). All artists must be available for the full 45 days from 1 February to 15 March 2027 and must be from eligible countries (EU countries, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply. The working and living spaces are wheelchair accessible, though reaching RAMDAM by public transport can be difficult for disabled people; accessibility support is available via Culture Moves Europe for residents with disabilities. ALLOWANCES: EUR 1,350 per resident for subsistence; travel allowance EUR 400 under 5,000 km or EUR 800 from 5,000 km (travel under 600 km must be by green means of transport unless disability or island residence requires otherwise); green mobility top-up EUR 400 per resident for non-plane travel over 600 km; visa top-up EUR 120; outermost regions/OCT top-up EUR 175; family top-up EUR 200 per child under 18 (housing capacity is limited). TO APPLY: applications in English or French; download the residency project and administrative form documents and email the complete application to contact@ramdamcda.org with subject line 'extended possibilities - 2027' (no WeTransfer, no important information in the email body; reduce file sizes; a third document may be added to help understand the work). Confirmation within 7 working days; shortlisted applicants have video calls with the residency committee in mid-August. Deadline: Tuesday 14 July 2026, 11:59 p.m. NOTE: the call is arbitrarily limited to the first 40 applications received (still open as of 1 July 2026), so apply early. Made possible by the European programme Culture Moves Europe.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Innovate Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Innovate Artist Grants

Location: Online (applications accepted worldwide)

Award: USD 1,800 unrestricted grant (two awarded per cycle: one to a visual artist, one to a photographer) USD 35 application fee

Duration: One-time unrestricted award; quarterly cycles (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall)

Deadline: 17 Sep 2026

Innovate Grant awards two unrestricted USD 1,800 grants every quarter, one to a visual artist and one to a photographer, along with up to 18 honorable mentions who are featured and promoted on the organization's website. The award is unrestricted, so recipients can apply the funds toward new projects, materials, studio rent, travel, or other needs. Grant cycles run four times a year (Winter, Spring, Summer, and Fall). Visual artists and photographers aged 18 and older from anywhere in the world, both US and international, are eligible to apply. Applications require contact details, a grant category, and 3 to 10 images of work, plus a USD 35 application fee. The Spring 2026 cycle closed on June 18, 2026, and the Summer 2026 cycle follows on the regular quarterly schedule.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Giancarlo DiTrapano Foundation: The Finch Manuscript Lab 2026
item.description:
Organisation: The Giancarlo DiTrapano Foundation for Literature and the Arts

Location: Finch Country House, Serramezzana, Italy; open to legal residents of the 39 Culture Moves Europe eligible countries, excluding Italy

Award: EUR 2,280 stipend for the residency period (EUR 30 per day in residence, paid weekly), plus travel allowance of EUR 400 (under 5,000 km) or EUR 800 (over 5,000 km), an additional EUR 400 green-travel top-up (e.g. trains instead of planes), and EUR 200 additional support for parents of children under 18. Supported by the Goethe Institute and Culture Moves Europe.

Duration: 2.5 months, 1 October to 15 December 2026

Deadline: 10 Jul 2026

The Finch Manuscript Lab is a 2.5-month residency supporting writers completing book-length manuscripts, hosted at the Finch Country House in Serramezzana, Italy, and designed as the final sprint in the marathon of writing a book. It offers living and work space, writing time in a tranquil setting, creative community, and professional development including manuscript consultations, craft discussions, and publishing seminars. Residency dates: 1 October to 15 December 2026; residents will be selected by 30 July 2026. FUNDING: thanks to the support of the Goethe Institute and Culture Moves Europe, all selected residents receive a stipend of EUR 2,280 for the residency period (EUR 30 per day in residence, paid weekly), a travel allowance of EUR 400 for distances under 5,000 km from Serramezzana or EUR 800 for distances over 5,000 km, an additional EUR 400 for green travel (e.g. taking trains instead of planes), and additional support of EUR 200 for parents of children under 18. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be over 18 and a legal resident of one of the 39 Culture Moves Europe eligible countries, excluding Italy; this includes all EU member states other than Italy and 13 participating non-EU countries (Albania, Andorra, Armenia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Iceland, Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Norway, Serbia, and Tunisia). APPLICATION (via Submittable, in order of importance): a short sample of past work (any length, though only the first 30 pages may be read); a brief note, up to one page, explaining the manuscript or book project to be worked on in residency, where you are in the creation process, and why you are interested; an excerpt of the book project not exceeding 30 pages (prose, poetry, mixed media, photography, or any other form of artist's book is acceptable); and a CV in English. NOTE ON DEADLINE: the foundation's text gives the open call as 15 June to 10 July 2026, while the Submittable page lists a close of Wednesday 15 July 2026 at 12:00 PM; apply by 10 July to be safe.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wassaic Project 2027 Summer Exhibition Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: The Wassaic Project

Location: Wassaic, New York, United States (in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills).

Award: A modest honorarium for all accepted artists (part of the organization's effort toward W.A.G.E. certification). Artists creating site-specific installations are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship and a no-fee residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Duration: The 2027 Summer Exhibition runs free and open to the public every weekend, 1 May to 12 September 2027. Optional fellowship residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

The Wassaic Project is holding its annual Open Call for the 2027 Summer Exhibition, for artists of all mediums, including 2D work, sculpture, video, new media, site-specific installation, performance, text, poems, essays, and publication-specific work. Selected work is showcased alongside a diverse range of pieces and performances in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills; the 2027 Summer Exhibition will be free and open to the public every weekend from 1 May to 12 September 2027. In an effort toward W.A.G.E. certification, all accepted artists receive a modest honorarium to participate. Artists interested in creating a site-specific installation are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship to help realize their work; Fellows are offered a no-fee residency for 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027 (artists interested in making site-specific work should still apply regardless of whether they are interested in or able to be in residence). The Wassaic Project cultivates and supports community for emerging and professional contemporary artists, writers, and other creatives, with a residency program housed in historic landmark buildings that hosts 10 artists each month in the heart of a rural community. APPLICATION: requires application forms, a CV, and an artwork/portfolio; submitted via SlideRoom. Application fee: USD 25 (no participation fee or additional fees). Deadline 10 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Sonic Connections Open Call 2027 (Villa Filanda Antonini)
item.description:
Organisation: Villa Filanda Antonini (VFA)

Location: Villorba, Italy (a former silk mill). Addressed to artists and professionals NOT currently residing in Italy.

Award: Fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary, plus a EUR 1,000 residency stipend.

Duration: One-month residency, 1-31 March 2027.

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Villa Filanda Antonini (VFA) launches its Spring Open Call to select up to 4 artists and professionals for a one-month residency in March 2027 on the theme 'Sonic Connections', focused on experimental music, sound art, and performances. Hosted at Villa Filanda Antonini, a former silk mill in Villorba, Italy, the project brings together four international artists and professionals to examine the relationship between architecture, landscape, and collective sonic experience. ADDRESSED TO: artists, performers, curators, designers, and professionals not currently residing in Italy. GRANT & BENEFITS: a fully funded residency covering mentoring, accommodation, travel costs, production costs, and a daily salary, plus a EUR 1,000 residency stipend. WHEN: 1-31 March 2027. HOW TO APPLY: complete the application form and attach a CV, portfolio, project proposal, and letter of reference. The VFA residency program has been selected under conditions by Culture Moves Europe, a project funded by the European Union and implemented by the Goethe-Institut. Deadline: 19 July 2026, 23:59 CEST.

Open call details

item.title: Club Nothing: Community Music Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Award: USD 40,000 fund total; four individuals or collectives each receive USD 10,000 to produce a club event, series, or festival. No application fee.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. Learn more and apply at nothing.tech/club-nothing (details also posted at https://nothing.community/d/59214-want-10000-to-throw-a-party). See full terms at nothing.tech/club-nothing/terms-and-conditions.

Open call details

item.title: Call to Artists: Walden Fire Station and Library (Calgary Arts Development)
item.description:
Organisation: Calgary Arts Development (CADA)

Location: Walden community, Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Open to local and national (Canadian) professional artists/artist teams.

Award: Total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes) for one selected artist/artist team. The artist fee for the total project is CAD 68,750 + GST. Budget breakdown can be adapted to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation that a minimum 20% of the budget go to the artist's fee; the total allocated budget will not be increased.

Duration: Concept and design from September 2026 to end of July 2027; fabrication and installation (Phase 2) from the second half of 2027 into 2028.

Deadline: 28 Jul 2026

Calgary Arts Development (CADA) is seeking an artist or artist team to join The City of Calgary's Walden Fire Station and Library project team to create permanent public artwork(s) for a combined fire station and library planned for the community of Walden. One artist/artist team will be selected with a total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes); the opportunity is open to local and national professional artists/artist teams working in various media. ELIGIBILITY: local and national mid-career to late-career professional public artists and artist teams with a minimum of five years' previous experience creating artwork in the public realm; they must have at least three outdoor permanent public art installations, each with a lifespan of over 10 years and a minimum project budget of CAD 150,000 (see the Canada Council for the Arts definition of a professional artist). Walden does not currently have an active community association, so the project requires experience in artist-led community engagement and a strong understanding of alternative outreach. Artists must be available to meet the project timelines from September 2026 to the end of concept and design by July 2027; fabrication and installation take place in Phase 2, starting in the second half of 2027 and continuing into 2028. REWARD: total project budget CAD 275,000 (artist fee CAD 68,750 + GST); CADA is open to adapting the budget breakdown to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation of a minimum 20% to the artist's fee, though the total budget will not be increased. TIMELINE: applications open 16 June 2026 (9am MT); pre-recorded information session posted early July; application deadline 28 July 2026 (no applications accepted after 1pm MT); shortlisted interviews mid-September; results late September 2026. Eligibility questions: walden@calgaryartsdevelopment.com.

Open call details

item.title: Genesis Mission AI Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service (with Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science)

Location: United States. Fellows are forward deployed to US Department of Energy national labs (initial partners: Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory). Remote or in-person; on-site travel at least once a quarter. Must be eligible to work in the US (no visa sponsorship; US green card holders eligible).

Award: Prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000.

Duration: 9-12 months, starting fall 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Horizon Institute for Public Service, Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science are partnering to launch a fellowship that embeds technical AI talent across the US Department of Energy national labs to advance the goals of the Genesis Mission, DOE's flagship science and technology initiative launched in late 2025. The Genesis Mission identifies 26 national science and technology challenges critical to US leadership and envisions a national platform connecting supercomputers, experimental facilities, AI systems, and unique datasets, with DOE's 17 national labs playing a key role. Genesis Mission AI Fellows are forward deployed to DOE national labs to bring frontier AI expertise to high-priority scientific challenges. As core members of the technical staff on a Genesis Mission project, fellows work alongside national lab scientists and partners and apply AI to national priorities, from building cutting-edge models of plasma dynamics in pursuit of controlled fusion to advancing state-of-the-art models for foundational materials discovery. Initial national lab partners include Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, and the Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory; additional partners may be announced later. COMPENSATION/DURATION: 9-12 months starting fall 2026, with a prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000; fellows are expected to travel to their national lab site at least once a quarter but may work on-site if they prefer. ELIGIBILITY: competitive applicants should have at least 2 years of industry experience building AI models or agents (those with industry experience building AI applications or data infrastructure will also be considered); scientific domain expertise is not required, but any placement will involve some training and study in a specific scientific discipline or energy topic, supported by expert colleagues at the host institution. All fellows must be eligible to work in the United States for the duration of the placement; US employment visas cannot be sponsored, but US permanent residents (green card holders) are eligible. APPLICATION: initial form requires a CV or resume (max 2 pages) and short-answer questions on motivations and experiences (max 200 words); successful applicants complete interviews through partner organizations and national labs, and selected fellows are matched to partner labs by scientific domain and availability. The review uses evidence-based best practices including blind grading of application materials. TIMELINE: application deadline 31 July 2026; final fellow selection August-September 2026; expected start fall 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Northeastern Illinois University (NEIU), Art + Design Department

Location: Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center, Northeastern Illinois University, Chicago, Illinois, United States. In-person; semester-long access to NEIU facilities.

Award: USD 800 honorarium, plus a semester of full access to NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to the project (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture); supplies can be requested with guidance and approval from Studio Area personnel. Visiting Artists may also exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery.

Duration: One semester. Residency Session 1: 25 January - 14 May; Session 2: 30 August - 11 December.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency strives to create visible connections between Northeastern Illinois University's diverse population of art and design students and working artists who embody these identities, providing insights and inspiration through a dynamic exchange of ideas and techniques. Visiting Artists should demonstrate a commitment to their artistic practice and an interest in engaging with NEIU's students and creative community. Each Visiting Artist is awarded an honorarium and has full access for a semester to the NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to their project; over the residency they showcase their work in a public academic forum, lead a hands-on workshop, and participate in a Portfolio Review Day for NEIU's Art + Design students. FACILITIES (in the Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center) include a Design Studio with the largest collection of Riso drums at any Chicago university, a Fab Lab (FDM and resin 3D printers, large-format CNC router, vinyl cutters, laser cutters), a Ceramic Studio (throwing wheels, glaze room, electric and gas kilns, raku area), a Letterpress Studio (two Vandercook presses, metal and wood type), a Sculpture Studio (wood shop, clay modeling and spray painting areas), a Photography Studio (analog and digital, plus a lighting studio), and fully equipped Painting, Drawing, and Printmaking studios. BENEFITS: USD 800 honorarium; facility access based on project focus (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture), with supplies requestable subject to approval; and the opportunity to exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery. PARTICIPATION: lead a 2-hour hands-on workshop for 15-20 students on a topic relevant to the project; give an hour-long artist talk with Q&A; and serve as a guest panelist for a day-long portfolio review. KEY DATES: open call 2 June - 9 August; emailed acceptances in October; Residency Session 1 runs 25 January - 14 May and Session 2 runs 30 August - 11 December. Apply via the JotForm application. Deadline 9 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Digital Democracy Centre x TrygFonden Fellowship Program 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Democracy Centre (University of Southern Denmark), with support from TrygFonden

Location: Odense, Denmark (University of Southern Denmark). Flexible mix of in-office stays at the DDC in Odense and remote periods; stays planned to the Danish working calendar.

Award: Either a salary subsidy or a stipend to help with travel and/or housing; specific benefits (financial support, access to DDC research resources, flexible working spaces, speaker series, networks) vary by agreement between the fellow, DDC, and partnering organizations. Applicants describe the posts they seek support for (flights, housing, research costs, salary compensation, stipend, etc.).

Duration: Flexible; typically 2-6 months (minimum two months, can include shorter DDC stays and remote periods), longer in exceptional cases. Fellowships start anywhere in 2027.

Deadline: 01 Sep 2026

The Digital Democracy Centre (DDC) at the University of Southern Denmark, with support from TrygFonden, is calling for applications for its 2027 fellowship cohort. The program fosters collaboration between researchers, journalists, and policymakers exploring the impact of digital technology and AI on media, politics, and democracy. Fellows collaborate with a self-selected DDC mentor to generate new knowledge, contribute to public debate, develop networks, and produce tangible outreach outputs such as policy briefs, educational packages, and talks. THREE FELLOWSHIP TYPES: (1) academic fellows researching digital technologies and democracy; (2) journalist fellows covering digital technology and democracy; (3) policy fellows contributing to the regulatory debate. Target group: researchers, journalism graduates, and policymakers in research/educational institutions, media organizations, ministries, NGOs, etc. PRIORITIES: projects with clear relevance to Denmark or Danish society (including comparative/international projects yielding insights relevant to a Danish context); in 2027, projects related to DDC's Hybrid Warfare Initiative (societal resilience to new forms of international conflict, with a focus on digital technologies and AI) are also prioritized. EXPECTATIONS: applicants must secure confirmation of mentorship from their desired DDC mentor BEFORE applying (applications without a confirmed mentor are automatically rejected; PhD students and research assistants are generally not eligible as mentors, and Sten Schaumburg-Muller is unavailable as a mentor for the 2027 call); applicants must also coordinate support from their current supervisors/managers. Fellows engage in DDC activities, seminars/workshops, and public outreach, and submit a short report within two months of finishing. APPLICATION (in English): preliminary project title, fellowship type, brief project description (max 1000 chars), detailed description and timeline (max 2400 chars, including duration, confirmed mentor, and remote/in-office work plan), description of activities and collaboration with external practice partners (max 1000 chars), expected product/impact/output (max 500 chars), a CV (max 5 pages), and an overview of the posts for which financial support is sought. Submit the application form (PDF or Word) plus CV as PDFs to ddc@sdu.dk with subject line 'DDC / TrygFonden Fellowship application'. Deadline 1 September 2026; decisions communicated no later than 1 October 2026.

Open call details

item.title: 100 Voices of Florida Film Grant
item.description:
Organisation: 100 Voices of Florida

Location: Florida, United States

Award: Up to USD 5,000 (unrestricted) USD 25 application fee with a Florida connection, USD 35 without

Duration: One-time grant

Deadline: 20 Sep 2026

100 Voices of Florida is a grant for filmmakers and screenwriters that awards up to USD 5,000 in unrestricted funds, which winners can use for finishing funds, festival submission fees, cast and crew reimbursements, production, or anything else. Applicants submit either a short film (under twenty minutes) or a script (twenty pages or less); films in other languages must include accurate English subtitles, and scripts must be in English. The grant is open to writers, directors, and producers who are at least 18 years old, and creative teams may submit, though only one entrant receives the award. A panel of judges with professional filmmaking experience evaluates entries, and the winner also receives a meeting with an industry professional. The deadline for the current cycle is September 20, 2026.

item.title: Hayama Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Hayama Artist Residency

Location: Hayama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan

Award: Fully funded: roundtrip airfare to Tokyo (Narita or Haneda), shared accommodation for 4 weeks, and USD 800 for meals and local transportation. Group exhibition at KOKI ARTS gallery in Tokyo. USD 95 application fee for first-time applicants

Duration: 4 weeks

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The Hayama Artist Residency introduces visual artists from around the world to Japanese culture and offers them their first gallery exhibition in Japan. The four-week residency takes place in Hayama, a coastal town in Kanagawa Prefecture about 1.5 hours from Tokyo. Selected artists receive roundtrip airfare to Tokyo, shared accommodation with one other resident, and USD 800 for meals and local transportation. A core element is a group exhibition during the stay, hosted in partnership with KOKI ARTS, a contemporary gallery in the Bakurocho art district of Tokyo. The program is open to visual artists over 21 working in any medium, based anywhere in the world, and applications are reviewed by the director and an international selection committee. The June 2027 cycle has an application deadline of September 30, 2026, with notification by December 15, 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Steps for Individuals 2026 (Arts Council of Wales)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Wales

Location: Wales, United Kingdom. For artists and creatives based in Wales.

Award: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs). Applicants can apply for up to 90% of the developmental cost; the remaining 10% must come from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both).

Duration: Supports a stage of professional development; allow a minimum of 10 working weeks between the deadline and the project start date.

Deadline: 29 Jul 2026

Creative Steps funds the professional, business and organisational development of artists who have experienced barriers to careers within the arts. Creative Steps for Individuals is open to individual artists and creatives who identify as being Ethnically and Culturally diverse, D/deaf, disabled or neurodivergent, groups that are underrepresented in Arts Council of Wales funding programmes due to barriers to entry. Applicants must have demonstrable experience as an artist or creative professional; those without may still be supported on the basis of artistic potential by submitting a portfolio, CV or examples of work, discussed with a Relationship Manager. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: career development, NOT creating new work or project delivery. Examples include peer-to-peer learning, training courses (not formal/vocational education), building networks, mentorship/coaching, business development/social media/marketing advice, website development/marketing materials/equipment or materials, and setting up a new organisation. Budgets may include the applicant's own time, freelancer costs (paid at standard industry rates), mentor/consultant costs, access costs, and childcare/caregiving costs. AMOUNT: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs); up to 90% of the developmental cost, with the remaining 10% from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both, confirmed by letters of support). No application fee. APPLY: applicants must express interest at least 3 weeks before the deadline to receive support from an Application Coach or Access Support coordinator; an application form is provided by a Relationship Manager. The fund runs in rounds; the next round (Round 2 2026) has an Expression of Interest cut-off of 8 July 2026 and an application deadline of 29 July 2026. Later rounds: Round 3 (EOI 30 September, deadline 21 October 2026) and Round 4 (EOI 10 February, deadline 3 March 2027).

Open call details

Project Funding for Individuals, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds project-based creative activities across all artistic disciplines. item.title: SIAP: Artists' International Development Fund 2026 (Arts Council NI)

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland; international development takes place abroad with overseas partners/hosts.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Early-stage international development opportunities; residencies supported for a maximum of 4 weeks.

The Artists' International Development Fund (AIDF), part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds early-stage international development opportunities for individual, freelance and self-employed artists to build networks abroad. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, travel, residencies (up to 4 weeks), technical fees, venue hire, and equipment, with IT devices capped at GBP 1,200 for laptops/PCs and GBP 500 for tablets. item.title: NEU Commission 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Page Not Found

Location: The Netherlands; open to non-EEA-national students enrolled at Dutch art academies.

Award: EUR 2,000 each to three commissioned writers, paid as an advance in September 2026.

Duration: One-time commission; commissioned texts published at a later date

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Page Not Found announces the third edition of the NEU Commission, which intends to support the emergence of alternative voices within contemporary art. It targets a group currently under-represented in Europe: art students with limited financial means who are required to provide a guarantee for their visa and to pay high non-EU tuition fees, economical barriers to their participation in art and knowledge production in The Netherlands. Page Not Found will commission three art students from non-European countries to each write an essay or a free-form text in English. Each selected writer receives EUR 2,000 for the commission, provided as an advance payment in September 2026; the commissioned texts are published at a later date. OPEN CALL: only students enrolled in Dutch art academies who are NOT nationals of the European Economic Area are eligible. The commissioned text does not need to be submitted when answering the open call; the jury selects recipients based on their previous writing and financial need. Review the Rules of the Open Call and the FAQ before applying. Deadline: Friday 31 July 2026, 23:59 CEST (Amsterdam).

Open call details

Location: Rome, Italy

The Prisma Art Prize is an international award for painting and two-dimensional artistic research based in Rome. The 20th edition selects emerging and established artists working in painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, and traditional mixed media for a group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, opening November 5, 2026. Winners share an annual cash fund of EUR 2,000 distributed across quarterly calls and receive inclusion in the collective catalogue plus artist interviews. Award: Stipend up to USD 30,000

Duration: Up to one year

The Dedalus Foundation, established by artist Robert Motherwell, awards a Senior Fellowship supporting critical and historical studies related to painting, sculpture, and allied arts of the twentieth century. Location: Banner, Wyoming, USA

Duration: Approximately 3 weeks (arrive on the 15th, depart the morning of the 7th of the following month)

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The Jentel Artist Residency offers approximately three-week residencies in the foothills of the Bighorn Mountains near Banner, Wyoming, giving artists, writers, and composers dedicated time for creative work. Sessions run from mid-January through early December, hosting six residents at a time. It is open to visual artists in any medium, writers of fiction, creative nonfiction, or poetry, and composers across musical genres. Residents receive a private bedroom, a separate studio, shared living and kitchen space, and a USD 100 weekly stipend to help cover food and personal expenses. Applicants must be at least 25 years old and be U.S. citizens or foreign nationals whose primary residence is in the U.S. during the application year, and may not be enrolled full-time in a degree program. There is a USD 30 non-refundable application fee, with deadlines of September 15 for Winter and Spring sessions and January 15 for Summer and Fall sessions.

item.title: Feminist Gaming Fellowship: Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Feminist Gaming Project

Location: Remote across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK

Award: EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow (paid half at the start, half on completion)

Duration: Three months, August to October 2026; minimum ~6 hours per week (~75 hours total)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

The Feminist Gaming Project is opening applications for its first Fellowship cohort: five fellows from different creative and research fields spending three months developing 3D assets, tools, and documentation for its Open Access Asset Library (OAL), a free, open repository of inclusive 3D content created by, with, and for intersectionally feminist and diverse communities. Where mainstream marketplaces default to hyper-athletic, idealized, or hyper-sexualized characters, the OAL offers average bodies, real postures, everyday objects, and the kinds of lives most games leave out (pregnancy and postpartum, care work, ageing, disability, domestic labor). This first cohort is dedicated to 3D content. Supported by the Creative Industries Fund NL and working across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK, the project challenges exclusionary norms in how games are made. The fellowship offers a EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow over three months (half at start, half on completion); mentorship from the core team and advisors in feminist design, game studies, 3D production, and digital culture; a peer community across NL, TR, DE, and the UK with biweekly meetings; visibility through a travelling exhibition program, publications, and public events; and authorship credited in the OAL under its license terms. Five roles are recruited (one fellow each): 3D Character Artist for inclusive base meshes; 3D Character Artist for pregnancy and postpartum assets; 3D Animator for neutral and everyday movement; 3D Modeller for domestic and care-labor environments; and Researcher/Writer for documentation and the representation framework; applicants may rank up to two. Open to 3D artists, animators, modellers, designers, researchers, and writers committed to inclusive and intersectional representation in games; prior game-industry experience is not required, and emerging creators, underrepresented practitioners, and researchers at the intersection of games, gender, and culture are especially encouraged, particularly those based in or connected to NL, TR, DE, or the UK. To apply, email feministgamingproject@gmail.com with a short statement (max ~500 words), a portfolio or work samples (a link is fine), and confirmation of availability for the three-month period; name the role(s) in the subject line or statement. The project explicitly asks applicants to write statements in their own voice rather than using AI-generated text. Indicative timeline: applications close 12 July 2026; selection and notification late July; fellowship period August to October 2026; showcase and publication November to December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

CIRCLE Fiction Orbit is a one-week retreat in Herceg Novi, Montenegro for up to six fiction feature film projects led by teams of women and gender-expansive filmmakers developing their second, third, or fourth narrative feature. Participants work with leading experts on script development, legal and production strategy, career positioning, and receive psychological support throughout the creative process. Applicants must identify as a woman or gender-expansive filmmaker, work as a screenwriter, director, or producer, have completed at least one feature, and submit a project with a producer attached. Accommodation and meals are provided, but travel is not covered, and a participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies. At least one 50 percent scholarship is available annually for those with demonstrated financial need. The third edition runs October 25 to November 1, 2026, with applications due July 10, 2026.

Loghaven Artist Residency offers practicing artists time, space, and resources on ninety wooded acres in Knoxville, Tennessee. Fellows stay in five rehabilitated historic log cabins with access to purpose-built studio space. item.description:
Organisation: The Center for Fiction

Location: New York City, USA

Award: USD 5,000 grant plus writers studio space, mentorship, agent introductions, and community programming

Duration: One year (the fellowship year)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Center for Fiction / Susan Kamil Emerging Writer Fellowship annually supports nine early-career, New York City-based fiction writers at a pivotal point in their careers. Each fellow receives a 5,000 USD grant, dedicated space and time to write in the Writers Studio, personalized editorial mentorship, opportunities to meet literary agents, and community programming. Applicants must be current residents of one of the five boroughs and must remain in New York City for the entire fellowship year. An emerging writer is defined as someone who has not yet had a novel or short story collection published by a major or independent publisher and who is not currently under contract for a work of fiction; students enrolled in a degree-granting program during the fellowship year are not eligible. item.title: MSB Interpretive Panels: Public Art Commission (Creative Philadelphia)
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Philadelphia (Office of Arts, Culture and the Creative Economy)

Location: Municipal Services Building, Center City, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States. Philadelphia-based artists strongly encouraged; artists from outside the region considered. Multiple in-person site visits required (travel costs covered within the project budget).

Award: Total project budget of USD 250,000, which must cover: artist fees (18% design fee), design and engineering, fabrication and installation, structural footings/anchoring, travel and site visits, AR development (software, hosting, content production), insurance (1%), community engagement participation, and a service warranty keeping content compatible with new mobile OS versions for a minimum of 10 years. No application fees.

Duration: Permanent public artwork commission; two-part selection process.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Creative Philadelphia announces a call to commission a series of permanent interpretive panels with integrated augmented reality (AR) components at the Municipal Services Building in Center City Philadelphia. The commission is part of a broader initiative that includes an upcoming public sculpture of Dr. Sadie T.M. Alexander by artist Vinnie Bagwell. Inspired by Alexander's groundbreaking legacy in public service, these interpretive panels will celebrate the City of Philadelphia's municipal employees (past, present, and future) and provide deeper context for understanding Dr. Alexander's impact; they will be located in front of the building's landscaped areas, flanking the upcoming sculpture, and are intended to complement and support the overall site experience. SCOPE: an artist team will design and fabricate four (4) permanent, sculptural interpretive panels incorporating augmented reality (AR) elements to expand storytelling beyond the physical surface of the artwork. ELIGIBILITY: all interested artists are invited to apply; Philadelphia-based artists are strongly encouraged, though artists from outside the region will be considered; multiple in-person site visits are required and associated travel costs must be covered within the project budget. SELECTION: a two-part process: Part 1 submission of qualifications; Part 2 finalist proposals and interviews. Up to three (3) finalists are invited to submit short concept proposals and attend an interview; at no point should artists submit design proposals. BUDGET: USD 250,000 total (see amount for the required cost breakdown). An Artist Informational Meeting is held via Zoom on 30 June, 6:00-7:00pm. Application questions: paige.phillips@phila.gov. Apply via Submittable. Deadline 12 July 2026 (submissions close Monday 13 July 2026, 6:00 AM).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
